notes: Structured data preserves full source text in description fields and extracts engine-facing indices heuristically.
Transitions are candidates unless marked in manual_transitions; map-derived geometry should supersede candidate inference when available.
Public narration must continue to route hidden_or_dm_facts through Layer 2 before reveal.

treasure_items.name: DL1: Dragons of Despair — Rules Engine Reference

source.heading: DL1: Dragons of Despair — Rules Engine Reference

treasure_items.name: THE BLUE CRYSTAL STAFF (Crystal of Mishakal)

source.heading: THE BLUE CRYSTAL STAFF (Crystal of Mishakal)

treasure_items.description: The central MacGuffin of the entire module. Carved from a single piece of blue crystal. About 5 feet long; 2-inch-diameter shaft. Ornamentally bladed head with a gem in its center.
treasure_refs: The central MacGuffin of the entire module. Carved from a single piece of blue crystal. About 5 feet long; 2-inch-diameter shaft. Ornamentally bladed head with a gem in its center.

treasure_items.name: Origin
treasure_items.description: Although Riverwind does not remember, a manifestation of the Goddess Mishakal gave him the staff in the Forsaken Lands.
treasure_refs: Although Riverwind does not remember, a manifestation of the Goddess Mishakal gave him the staff in the Forsaken Lands.

treasure_items.name: Intelligence and Alignment
treasure_items.description:
- **Intelligent artifact**
- **Alignment:** Lawful Good
- **Ego:** 10
- Can only be used by Lawful Good characters
- Non-LG characters who try to wield the staff: **take 4d6 points of electrical damage**
- An LG person may use the staff to heal the electrical damage it has caused to others
treasure_refs: Non-LG characters who try to wield the staff: **take 4d6 points of electrical damage**
An LG person may use the staff to heal the electrical damage it has caused to others
checks_and_rolls: Non-LG characters who try to wield the staff: **take 4d6 points of electrical damage**

treasure_items.name: Powers
treasure_items.description:
**1. Striking:** Acts as a staff of striking (1–3 charges per use)

**2. Clerical spells** (2 charges per level of spell):
| Spell Level | Spells Available |
|-------------|-----------------|
| 1st | command, cure light wounds, remove fear |
| 3rd | continual light, cure blindness, cure disease, remove curse |
| 5th | cure critical wounds, raise dead |
| 7th | restoration, resurrection |

**3. Teleportation:** At its own discretion (using 15 charges)

**4. Dragon breath deflection:** In a 10' radius (10 charges per use)

rows.Spells Available: command, cure light wounds, remove fear
continual light, cure blindness, cure disease, remove curse

labeled_rules.label: 1. Striking
labeled_rules.text: Acts as a staff of striking (1–3 charges per use)

labeled_rules.label: 4. Dragon breath deflection
labeled_rules.text: In a 10' radius (10 charges per use)

treasure_refs: **1. Striking:** Acts as a staff of striking (1–3 charges per use)

treasure_items.name: Charges
treasure_items.description:
- Holds up to 20 charges at a time
- Regains 1 charge per day from the combined influence of Krynn's three moons
- Can be immediately and completely recharged at the statue of Mishakal (area 46b) by placing the staff in the statue's arms
treasure_refs: Can be immediately and completely recharged at the statue of Mishakal (area 46b) by placing the staff in the statue's arms

treasure_items.name: The Staff's Fate
treasure_items.description:
Upon completion of the Quest for the Disks of Mishakal — specifically, if a PC uses the staff to strike Khisanth in area 70k — the staff **shatters on contact**. Blue light bursts from it in brilliant spherical waves. The person who broke the staff disappears in light. The blue wave shakes the earth, covers the dragon, and the dragon falls screaming into light. The cavern begins to collapse.

The person who broke the staff reappears at the statue of Mishakal (area 46b), unharmed with all HP restored. The staff becomes part of the statue. The PC who broke it now wears a **platinum Medallion of Faith** bearing the symbol of the goddess.

---
treasure_refs: Upon completion of the Quest for the Disks of Mishakal — specifically, if a PC uses the staff to strike Khisanth in area 70k — the staff **shatters on contact**. Blue light bursts from it in brilliant spherical waves. The person who broke the staff disappears in light. The blue wave shakes the earth, covers the dragon, and the dragon falls screaming into light. The cavern begins to collapse.

The person who broke the staff reappears at the statue of Mishakal (area 46b), unharmed with all HP restored. The staff becomes part of the statue. The PC who broke it now wears a **platinum Medallion of Faith** bearing the symbol of the goddess.

treasure_items.name: THE DISKS OF MISHAKAL
treasure_items.description: The primary objective of the adventure. Located in Khisanth's hoard at area 70k.
treasure_refs: The primary objective of the adventure. Located in Khisanth's hoard at area 70k.

treasure_items.name: Physical Description
treasure_items.description:
- Platinum disks, 18 inches in diameter
- Each disk 1/16 inch thick
- **160 plates in total**
- A bolt passes through one side of the plates, allowing each to swivel out and be viewed while keeping the stack together
- Each plate is engraved on both sides
treasure_refs: Platinum disks, 18 inches in diameter

treasure_items.name: Alignment Restriction
treasure_items.description:
- **LG or NG alignment:** May examine the plates freely
- **Other alignments:** Take **4d6 points of electrical damage** each time they try to touch or read the disks
treasure_refs: **Other alignments:** Take **4d6 points of electrical damage** each time they try to touch or read the disks
checks_and_rolls: **Other alignments:** Take **4d6 points of electrical damage** each time they try to touch or read the disks

treasure_items.name: What the Disks Contain
treasure_items.description:
Engraved knowledge about the ancient Gods of Good:
| God | Domain |
|-----|--------|
| **Paladine** | Rulership |
| **Majere** | Meditation and control |
| **Kiri-Jolith** | War and battle |
| **Mishakal** | Healing |
| **Habakuk** | Seas and animals |
| **Branchala** | Elves, forests, and music |

treasure_items.name: Effect
treasure_items.description:
Any cleric who reads this book and worships one of these gods may receive spells per **standard AD&D rules**. The book tells how to worship them and gain true clerical abilities. This **restores clerical spellcasting to Krynn**.

---

treasure_items.name: Creation
treasure_items.description:
The platinum Medallion of Faith is created when the Crystal Staff is shattered against Khisanth. It appears around the neck of the PC who broke the staff.

The medallion is the symbol of those who worship the true gods. Each medallion **magically creates another medallion of faith** for clerics of any good alignment who profess faith in the old gods of good. The created medallion bears the symbol of the god that the cleric professes, regardless of which symbol the original bears.
treasure_refs: The platinum Medallion of Faith is created when the Crystal Staff is shattered against Khisanth. It appears around the neck of the PC who broke the staff.

The medallion is the symbol of those who worship the true gods. Each medallion **magically creates another medallion of faith** for clerics of any good alignment who profess faith in the old gods of good. The created medallion bears the symbol of the god that the cleric professes, regardless of which symbol the original bears.

treasure_items.name: Powers
treasure_items.description:
- No powers beyond creation of additional medallions as described above
- Serves as a symbol of faith
- True believers wear them at all times, although never openly

---
treasure_refs: No powers beyond creation of additional medallions as described above

treasure_items.name: Wilderness and Surface
treasure_items.description:
| Area | Treasure | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| 7 | Silver bracer with 4 gems (500 gpv each) | Belongs to dead warrior Firehawk of Que-Shu; can be identified by Que-Shu Plainsmen |
| 46d | Magic-user scroll: 10th-level lightning bolt | Left on dais by attacking draconians |
| 47e | 5 gems, 1,000 gpv each | In small box at edge of floorless room |
| 47g | 6 swords +1 | One per skeleton coffin in treasury room |

treasure_items.name: Underground City Treasures
treasure_items.description:
| Area | Treasure | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| 55 | Steel doors (2) | 1,500 gpw each; worth 15,000 gp each in Solace, Haven, or Gateway |
| 57b | 500 clay Tsarothian Culli | Can pay Kathal's tax in area 57a; otherwise worthless |
| 65b | Longswords +2 (×3), Elven bow + 12 arrows +1 | Hidden behind baskets against west wall |
| 68k | Old map showing sewer access (69b) and route to dragon's lair (70k) | Found searching Highbulp's clothing piles |
| 70d | Among 30,000 worthless Culli (30% chance to find): two throwing daggers +3, gauntlets of climbing, spellbook (wizard lock, knock, invisibility), shield +1 | Search required |
| 70f | Pack with dried meats + map of the palace (areas 70a–70h) | In Bozak captain's room |
| **70k** | **Dragon's hoard** | **See below** |

---

treasure_items.description:

| **Disks of Mishakal** | Priceless | Primary quest objective; 160 platinum plates; see above |
| Gems | 56 gems @ 200 gp value each | Total: 11,200 gp equivalent |

| Platinum pieces | 1,000 pp | — |
| Ring of darkness | 1 | Projects darkness to a **100' radius** |

treasure_items.description:
- +3 protection
- +2 to hit (1d8 damage)
- Continual light: 1×/day
- Feather fall: 1×/day
- Personal item; begins with Raistlin

- Personal item; begins with Riverwind

treasure_items.name: CURRENCY QUICK REFERENCE (see DL1_01 for full details)

source.heading: CURRENCY QUICK REFERENCE (see DL1_01 for full details)

treasure_items.description:
All treasures found in Xak Tsaroth are in **Tsarothian clay Culli** (completely worthless in the present age) or in metal items with intrinsic value. The 30,000 Culli in area 70d are worthless except as the tax payment for the Clerk Spectral Minion in area 57a.

**Steel pieces = XP:** 1 steel = 1 XP for experience purposes.

**Gems and metals** have gpv/gpw values as noted in the standard AD&D exchange (1 gpv = 1 gp equivalent for experience and purchasing purposes, converted at the rate of 1 steel Emas = 50 sp in Seeker Lands).
treasure_refs: All treasures found in Xak Tsaroth are in **Tsarothian clay Culli** (completely worthless in the present age) or in metal items with intrinsic value. The 30,000 Culli in area 70d are worthless except as the tax payment for the Clerk Spectral Minion in area 57a.
**Steel pieces = XP:** 1 steel = 1 XP for experience purposes.

**Gems and metals** have gpv/gpw values as noted in the standard AD&D exchange (1 gpv = 1 gp equivalent for experience and purchasing purposes, converted at the rate of 1 steel Emas = 50 sp in Seeker Lands).
